title: Operator Precedence
subtitle: Order of operator execution
goal: Understand the order in which JavaScript evaluates operators.
explanation: When an expression has several operators, JavaScript does not always go left to right — it follows precedence rules.
Multiplication and division have higher precedence than addition and subtraction.
The order of execution can completely change the result of an expression.
When in doubt, use parentheses to make the code clearer.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: Imagine a line at a store. Some customers have priority and go first. Some operators run before others in the same way.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Beginners often expect JavaScript to always evaluate an expression left to right. In reality, it follows operator precedence rules.
